action.reasoning:
Observation: 28 iters used, 2 left, ~4h. Best safe_follow_steps=53 (iter 7), all 0/50. Latest iteration restored a simpler controller and got 36.3 — better than recent complex versions but still below best. With only 2 iters left and ~4h, I should make my final submission count.

Analysis: The current predict.py is the simplest version yet, and it works (36.3). The best score (53.12) was iter 7's slightly different controller. Given the consistent ~30-50 range with 0/50 success regardless of approach, marginal tweaks won't change the binary success outcome. Let me make a small tweak likely to help (increase TARGET_DIST safe-band forward speed to better match human velocity, increase turn gain) and submit final eval. Then done.

Decision: Submit eval on the current best checkpoint (the metric will be evaluated by the harness using predict.py). Since training is heuristic-only (no actual model), the checkpoint is just a placeholder. Submit eval one final time, then call done.
